name: Hydrogen
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "hydro" (water) and "genes" (creator), meaning water-former.

name: Helium
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "hēlios" (sun), as it was first observed in the solar spectrum.

name: Lithium
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "lithos" (stone), as it was discovered in a mineral.

name: Beryllium
symbolOrigin: From "beryl", a mineral from which it was first isolated.

name: Boron
symbolOrigin: From "borax", a mineral from which it was first isolated.

name: Carbon
symbolOrigin: From the Latin "carbo" (coal), as it was known in ancient times.

name: Nitrogen
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "nitron" (native soda) and "genes" (forming), meaning niter-forming.

name: Oxygen
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "oxys" (acid) and "genes" (forming), meaning acid-forming.

name: Fluorine
symbolOrigin: From the Latin "fluere" (to flow), as it was first observed in mineral flux.

name: Neon
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "neos" (new), as it was a new element discovered in 1898.

name: Sodium
symbolOrigin: From the English "soda" and the Latin "natron" (sodium carbonate), referring to the mineral natron.

name: Magnesium
symbolOrigin: From "Magnesia", a district in Thessaly, Greece, where the mineral magnesite was first found.

name: Aluminium
symbolOrigin: From the Latin "alumen" (bitter salt), referring to the mineral alum.

name: Silicon
symbolOrigin: From the Latin "silex" (flint), as it was first obtained from flint.

name: Phosphorus
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "phos" (light) and "phoros" (bearing), meaning light-bearer.

name: Sulfur
symbolOrigin: From the Latin "sulphur", meaning brimstone.

name: Chlorine
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "chloros" (greenish-yellow), referring to its color.

name: Argon
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "argos" (inactive), as it is chemically inert.

name: Potassium
symbolOrigin: From the English "potash" and the Latin "kalium" (from Arabic "al-qalyah"), referring to plant ashes.

name: Calcium
symbolOrigin: From the Latin "calx" (lime), as it was first isolated from lime.

name: Scandium
symbolOrigin: From "Scandia", the Latin name for Scandinavia, where it was first discovered.

name: Titanium
symbolOrigin: From the Titans of Greek mythology, symbolizing strength.

name: Vanadium
symbolOrigin: From "Vanadis", another name for the Norse goddess Freyja, due to the element’s colorful compounds.

name: Chromium
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "chroma" (color), due to the colorful compounds it forms.

name: Manganese
symbolOrigin: From the Latin "magnes" (magnet), as it was first discovered in magnetic ores.

name: Iron
symbolOrigin: From the Anglo-Saxon "iren", and the symbol "Fe" from the Latin "ferrum".

name: Cobalt
symbolOrigin: From the German "kobold" (goblin), as miners believed it contained harmful substances.

name: Nickel
symbolOrigin: From the German "kupfernickel" (false copper), as it resembled copper but was worthless.

name: Copper
symbolOrigin: From the Latin "cuprum", from "Cyprus", where it was mined in ancient times.

name: Zinc
symbolOrigin: From the German "zinke", meaning pointed, referring to its crystalline form.

name: Gallium
symbolOrigin: From "Gallia", the Latin name for France, honoring the discoverer’s homeland.

name: Germanium
symbolOrigin: From "Germania", the Latin name for Germany, honoring the discoverer’s homeland.

name: Arsenic
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "arsenikon" (masculine), referring to its potency.

name: Selenium
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "selene" (moon), as it was found alongside tellurium (named after Earth).

name: Bromine
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "bromos" (stench), due to its strong odor.

name: Krypton
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "kryptos" (hidden), as it was found in trace amounts.

name: Rubidium
symbolOrigin: From the Latin "rubidus" (deep red), due to the red emission lines in its spectrum.

name: Strontium
symbolOrigin: From "Strontian", a town in Scotland where the mineral was first discovered.

name: Yttrium
symbolOrigin: From "Ytterby", a village in Sweden where the mineral was first found.

name: Zirconium
symbolOrigin: From "zirkon", a mineral name, derived from Arabic "zarqūn" (cinnabar).

name: Niobium
symbolOrigin: From "Niobe", daughter of King Tantalus in Greek mythology, as it resembled tantalum.

name: Molybdenum
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "molybdos" (lead), as it was often confused with lead ores.

name: Technetium
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "technetos" (artificial), as it was the first element to be artificially produced.

name: Ruthenium
symbolOrigin: From "Ruthenia", the Latin name for Russia, honoring the discoverer’s homeland.

name: Rhodium
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "rhodon" (rose), due to the rose-red color of its compounds.

name: Palladium
symbolOrigin: From "Pallas", an asteroid discovered around the same time as the element.

name: Silver
symbolOrigin: From the Anglo-Saxon "seolfor" and the Latin "argentum", meaning bright.

name: Cadmium
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "kadmeia", referring to the ancient name for the mineral calamine.

name: Indium
symbolOrigin: From the indigo-blue line in its spectrum.

name: Tin
symbolOrigin: From the Anglo-Saxon "tin", and the symbol "Sn" from the Latin "stannum".

name: Antimony
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "antimonos" (not alone), as it was rarely found pure in nature.

name: Tellurium
symbolOrigin: From the Latin "tellus" (earth), as it was found in ores of gold.

name: Iodine
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "iodes", meaning violet, due to its violet vapor.

name: Xenon
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "xenos", meaning strange or foreign.

name: Caesium
symbolOrigin: From the Latin "caesius", meaning sky blue, referring to its bright blue spectral lines.

name: Barium
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "barys", meaning heavy, due to the high density of its compounds.

name: Lanthanum
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "lanthanein", meaning to lie hidden, reflecting its elusive nature.

name: Cerium
symbolOrigin: Named after the asteroid Ceres, discovered in 1801.

name: Praseodymium
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "prasios didymos", meaning green twin, due to its green-colored compounds.

name: Neodymium
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "neos didymos", meaning new twin, as it was a new element separated from didymium.

name: Promethium
symbolOrigin: Named after Prometheus, the Greek titan who stole fire from the gods.

name: Samarium
symbolOrigin: Named after the mineral samarskite, which was named after the Russian mine official Vasili Samarsky-Bykhovets.

name: Europium
symbolOrigin: Named after the continent Europe.

name: Gadolinium
symbolOrigin: Named after the Finnish chemist Johan Gadolin.

name: Terbium
symbolOrigin: Named after the Swedish village of Ytterby, where the mineral was first discovered.

name: Dysprosium
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "dysprositos", meaning hard to get, due to its rarity.

name: Holmium
symbolOrigin: Named after Stockholm, Sweden, the home city of the discoverers.

name: Erbium
symbolOrigin: Named after the Swedish village of Ytterby.

name: Thulium
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "Thule", meaning the northernmost part of the known world.

name: Ytterbium
symbolOrigin: Named after the Swedish village of Ytterby.

name: Lutetium
symbolOrigin: From the Latin "Lutetia", the ancient name for Paris.

name: Hafnium
symbolOrigin: From the Latin "Hafnia", meaning Copenhagen, where it was discovered.

name: Tantalum
symbolOrigin: From the Greek myth of King Tantalus, who was condemned to stand in water that receded when he tried to drink.

name: Tungsten
symbolOrigin: From the Swedish "tung sten", meaning heavy stone.

name: Rhenium
symbolOrigin: Named after the Rhine River in Europe.

name: Osmium
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "osme", meaning smell, due to its strong odor.

name: Iridium
symbolOrigin: From the Latin "iris", meaning rainbow, due to the colorful compounds it forms.

name: Platinum
symbolOrigin: From the Spanish "platina", meaning little silver.

name: Gold
symbolOrigin: From the Latin "aurum", meaning shining dawn.

name: Mercury
symbolOrigin: From the Latin "hydrargyrum", meaning liquid silver.

name: Thallium
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "thallos", meaning green shoot or twig, due to the green spectral line it emits.

name: Lead
symbolOrigin: From the Anglo-Saxon "leod", meaning people, and the symbol "Pb" from the Latin "plumbum".

name: Bismuth
symbolOrigin: From the German "Wismut", meaning white mass.

name: Polonium
symbolOrigin: Named after Poland, the homeland of Marie Curie.

name: Astatine
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "astatos", meaning unstable, due to its radioactivity.

name: Radon
symbolOrigin: From the Latin "radius", meaning ray, due to its radioactivity.

name: Francium
symbolOrigin: Named after France, where it was discovered.

name: Radium
symbolOrigin: From the Latin "radius", meaning ray, due to its intense radioactivity.

name: Actinium
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "aktinos", meaning ray, due to its radioactivity.

name: Thorium
symbolOrigin: Named after Thor, the Norse god of thunder.

name: Protactinium
symbolOrigin: From the Greek "protos", meaning first, and "actinium", due to its relation to actinium.

name: Uranium
symbolOrigin: Named after the planet Uranus.

name: Neptunium
symbolOrigin: Named after the planet Neptune.

name: Plutonium
symbolOrigin: Named after the dwarf planet Pluto.

name: Americium
symbolOrigin: Named after the Americas.

name: Curium
symbolOrigin: Named after Marie and Pierre Curie.

name: Berkelium
symbolOrigin: Named after the city of Berkeley, California.

name: Californium
symbolOrigin: Named after the state of California.

name: Einsteinium
symbolOrigin: Named after Albert Einstein.

name: Fermium
symbolOrigin: Named after Enrico Fermi.

name: Mendelevium
symbolOrigin: Named after Dmitri Mendeleev.

name: Nobelium
symbolOrigin: Named after Alfred Nobel.

name: Lawrencium
symbolOrigin: Named after Ernest O. Lawrence.

name: Rutherfordium
symbolOrigin: Named after Ernest Rutherford.

name: Dubnium
symbolOrigin: Named after the city of Dubna, Russia.

name: Seaborgium
symbolOrigin: Named after Glenn T. Seaborg.

name: Bohrium
symbolOrigin: Named after Niels Bohr.

name: Hassium
symbolOrigin: Named after the German state of Hesse.

name: Meitnerium
symbolOrigin: Named after Lise Meitner.

name: Darmstadtium
symbolOrigin: Named after the city of Darmstadt, Germany.

name: Roentgenium
symbolOrigin: Named after Wilhelm Röntgen.

name: Copernicium
symbolOrigin: Named after Nicolaus Copernicus.

name: Nihonium
symbolOrigin: Named after Japan (Nihon).

name: Flerovium
symbolOrigin: Named after Georgy Flerov.

name: Moscovium
symbolOrigin: Named after Moscow, Russia, near where the element was discovered.

name: Livermorium
symbolOrigin: Named after the Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory in California, USA.

name: Tennessine
symbolOrigin: Named after the U.S. state of Tennessee, home to Oak Ridge National Laboratory.

name: Oganesson
symbolOrigin: Named in honor of Russian physicist Yuri Oganessian.
